feed.title: SAP Community - SD (Sales and Distribution)
feed.subtitle: SD (Sales and Distribution) Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Bapi for Advanced Return Management order creation
entry.content:
Hello, 

I need to create from a Sales order through Bapi a return order based on the Advanced return order functionality.

Unfortunately actually we found only the BAPI to create Standard Return order but no BAPI for the ARM that can be used also to map the follow Up Activities. Can you please help us to understand if there is some functionality that fits our requirement?

Thanks

entry.title: Require Sending Plant & Receiving Plant details of STO
entry.content:
can a single report/Table define GRN details of a STO along with below details

PO no

Outbound Delivery

Sending Plant

Receiving Plant

GRN Qty

entry.title: Creating Sales Orders with Billing Plans and Delivery Dates via API
entry.content:
While working on an integration to create sales orders in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, I came across two relevant OData APIs provided by SAP:

  • OData V4: CE_SALESORDER_0001

  • OData V2: API_SALES_ORDER_SRV

I encountered some unexpected limitations and I am hoping you could help me with a best practice approach. 

The Problem

Goal:

  • Create a Sales Order via API

  • Include a billing plan on item level

  • Include the requested delivery date on item level

What I Found

V2 – API_SALES_ORDER_SRV

  • Supports billing plan at item level 

  • Does not support Requested Delivery Date at item level directly in the item payload 

    • Strangely enough, the “Create Sales Order” Fiori app does let you enter requested delivery dates per item manually

    • You can only set it via a second call to the schedule line entity (to_ItemScheduleLine)

V4 – CE_SALESORDER_0001

  • No support for billing plans 

  • Does support Requested Delivery Date per item

The Workaround I’m Using

Since I want both the billing plan and requested delivery date, I’m forced to:

  1. Use V2 API to create the Sales Order with Billing Plan.

  2. Make a second API call to /A_SalesOrderItemScheduleLine to update the RequestedDeliveryDate.

My Questions

  • Why is OData V4, a newer version, missing such key functionality like billing plans?
  • Is there a reason why requested delivery date is not available directly in the item payload in V2?
  • Are there other options or best practices to handle this more efficiently?
  • Is there a roadmap for V2? Or will changes only apply for newer versions?

entry.title: Issue with Returns Pulling Current MAP Instead of Original MAP
entry.content:
We're running into an issue where the MAP (Moving Average Price) on a return is being calculated using the current MAP, rather than the MAP that was in place at the time of the original sales document — even though the return is correctly linked to the original sales invoice.

For some context:
Our business frequently purchases the same product at different prices, which causes the MAP to fluctuate. In this particular case, a customer bought an item when the MAP was £17.33. We then restocked that item at a different price, and the MAP updated accordingly. When the customer later returned the item, the return document used the new MAP of £18 instead of the original £17.33.

Is there a way to configure the system so that returns pull the MAP from the original sales document, rather than using the current MAP at the time of return?

Any help or insight would be much appreciated!

entry.title: I cannot maintain Form routine field in IS-REA partner
entry.content: I cannot maintain Form routine field in IS-REA partner in J7LE. This field is not editable

entry.title: Require Current Date VK11 Pricing
entry.content:
Hello All,

Basically, Price in VK11 is maintained with From Date and To Date. But we need to develop a different auto generated Pricing report which should be based on current date, So I need the below marked Table-Field to write the FS. 

Please guide me to get the data.

Thanks in Advance.

entry.title: TCS Configuration for Scrap Sales in India (SAP Public Cloud) – Threshold Based Rate Application
entry.content:
Dear SAP Support,

We need guidance on the complete configuration steps for calculating TCS (Tax Collected at Source) on scrap sales in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud – India localization.

Our requirement is as follows:

  • 0.1% TCS should apply when the customer crosses the ₹50 lakhs threshold in the financial year.

  • 1% TCS should apply from the first scrap sale if no PAN is available (i.e., no threshold consideration).

  • This TCS should be calculated in the scrap sales order accordingly.

Kindly provide the detailed configuration steps, including:

  • Relevant Tax Code / Condition Type settings

  • Customer master data changes (if any)

  • Threshold control setup

  • Assignment in tax procedure or SSCUI details (if applicable)

This is critical for compliance and we request your urgent help.

Thanks & regards,
Vishal

entry.title: Material Substitution with Reason 0006 - Missing Confirmed Quantities
entry.content:
Dear Community,

I’d like to ask you about the following scenario.
We do material substitution in the sales order using substitution reason 0006 (ProdSel - Order only).

In the material determination records we give multiple alternative materials (to be selected according to availability), of which one has the MRP indicator set so that requirements for unconfirmed order quantities get created for that material.

In the customizing for the scope of ATP check we have the setting that at the end of RLT all order quantities should get confirmed.
However, in this substitution scenario SAP does not consider that setting. Instead, it considers only the ATP quantities up to the requested date and leaves the remaining quantity unconfirmed.

My question is: is there a way to get the confirmed quantity based on RLT for the item with the MRP-relevant material?

Thanks and regards
Helmut

entry.title: Setting of different Credit Limit Days as per different customer
entry.content:
Hello Gurus,

Can we set different Credit Limit days for different Customer, so that Business can decide what Maximum Pay Period to be provided to different customer as per their sales/payment record. If the Credit Limit day goes over, the Customer will be automatically Blocked for delivery.

Customer A: 90 days

Customer B: 100 days

Customer C: 110 days.

If this configuration is possible, could you please tell me the process.

entry.title: Manage Customer returns - greyed out refund control
entry.content:
In the app "Manage Customer Returns - Version 2", Once you choose the refund control "Decide later" in order to first execute the Returns delivery and inspection process, you can not go back to modify the refund control option from "Decide later" to any other option like "Create credit memo request / Create credit memo / No Refund".

Is there a reason why it is greyed out and not allowing to be "Decided later" if that makes more sense from a process perspective?

And is there anyway to overrun it and choose a specific refund control like the creating of a credit memo request or a credit memo.

Thank you all in advance for any answers or ideas!

entry.title: SD Sales schedule agreements VA31 automatic creation of delivery dates
entry.content:
How would you organize this process?

Office staff would like to create scheduling agreements.
For example, the same articles should always be delivered automatically on Mondays within a validity period.
Example: Hospital receives new food every Monday.

Variant 1: Create VA31 delivery schedules manually -> but this is a lot of work.
Such a button “Generate delivery schedule automatically until the end of the validity period”

Variant 2: Customer sends via EDI ?
Variant 3: FIORI “Import Sales Contract” with Excel.

Is there another variant?

Thank you!

entry.title: Sales order Creation from purchase order
entry.content:
We are automating the creation of sales order. We will receive a PO via idoc and CPI will convert it into ORDERS05 and with that we will create SO. Currently after all mapping we have created SO, but idoc is still in 51 status with error message 113 items displayed.

 

Can anyone highlight what could be the issue.

entry.title: Calculation technique in SAP SD
entry.content: Hi Gurus, I am looking at a way to replicate this invoice with these calculations in SAP SD as per requirement from client. Please share some advice on how to achieve this.

entry.title: Down Payment at header level of a Sales Order
entry.content:
Hello SAP team,

I have some questions regarding down payment at header of a sales order.

1. Is it possible to enable a billing plan at header of a sales order? If yes, how do i achieve this?

2. How does a down payment at header level of a sales order works?

3. Is it possible to use percentage based and not value based?

Thanks for your inputs

entry.title: Outbound delivery has been deleted but it was appearing in VF04 Transaction.
entry.content:
Outbound delivery has been deleted but it was appearing in VF04 Transaction. so how can we delete the entry in VF04. 

I have checked relevant tables LIKP, LIPS document not showing.

entry.title: Pricing error in item 000010
entry.content: While creating Invoice- Vf01 for my sales order. Invoice no generated and getting a notification like Pricing error in item 000010 and also net value is showing zero

entry.title: Issue with Availability/ATP despite confirmed qty in Sales order
entry.content:
Dears,
I’ve got the error message "Only 0,00 PC of material EC-09942-681 available" when trying to create a Delivery doc - even though the required stock is available and confirmed in the preceding Sales Order. 

For more details/analysis, please find attached 2 screenshots:

  • AvCheck_issue_VL01n.jpg - with the error message when processing Delivery doc.
  • AvCheck_issue_analysis.jpg - availability analysis from MD04, V_RA and V_V2

There are 2 orders with that material:

  • 4600122 with 1 qty, confirmed, and for this one a Delivery is currently required
  • 4400935 with 2 qty, also confirmed

It seems that the system confirmed quantities from both SOs (but only 1 PC is in the stock, MMBE result in the screenshot). When I run Backorder Processing V_V2, the system changes only Delivery Date for 4400935 order (but not confirmed qty) - see the screenshot with analysis.

The workaround is (1): to manually remove confirmed qty from order 4400935 (e.g. via V_RA), so the Delivery for 4600122 can be processed. Alternatively, (2): set 'AvailCkOff' for Delivery item category, for this case, in 0VLP transaction (negative stock is not allowed anyway). But I suppose that (1) neither (2) workaround is not a good practice or even not recommended.

Can you please advise what might be the root cause and how this can be handled properly - both for this case and to prevent similar issues in the future? Version: SAP ECC 6.0
Thanks in advance.

Regards
Krzysztof

entry.title: Change tax classification for substituted material sub-item
entry.content:
Hello,

I have a question, maybe it is standard how it works, but would like to check, is it possible to change the tax classification in the sales order item for a substituted material sub-item, currently for the main item the field is open/editable, but for the sub-item it is not, it is greyed out, so in case the tax classification of the sub-item has to be changed, can this be enabled for entry, are there options to do that?

Thank you!

entry.title: Step-by-step Configuration of Business Partner in SAP S/4 HANA
entry.content:
Configuration Guide: Creating a Business Partner with Custom Roles in SAP S/4HANA

This document provides a detailed, step-by-step configuration guide for creating a new Business Partner (BP) in SAP S/4HANA with custom roles, using the same number for both the Business Partner and Customer.

Overview of Key Terminologies

  • Business Partner Role Category: Groups similar business partner roles (e.g., Sales, Purchasing, etc.) and can be used for programming or categorization.
  • Business Partner View: Used to customize the field attributes (mandatory, optional, hidden) on the BP screen.
  • BP Grouping: Determines the number range for BPs and is essential to synchronize BP and Customer numbers. 

Configuration of Business Partner

Step 1: Defining the BP Role Categories

Path: SPRO>> IMG>>Cross Application Components>>SAP Business Partner>>Business Partner>>Basic Settings>>Business Partner Roles>>Define BP Roles - BP Role Categories

Define the BP role category: BP Role Categories are defined for Company Code, Sales, and Purchasing

ZGG000

ZLCU00

ZLCU01

Step 2: Defining Custom BP roles and assigning the BP Role Categories

Path: SPRO>> IMG>>Cross Application Components>>SAP Business Partner>>Business Partner>>Basic Settings>>Business Partner Roles>>Define BP Roles - BP Roles

Define BP Roles

BP roles for each function have been created, and each BP role is assigned to the BP role Category defined in the previous step.

BP Roles Defined:

ZGG000 - General BP Role

ZFCU00 - Assigned with the BP role Category ZLCU00 - Customer Company Code

ZFCU01 - Assigned with the BP role Category ZLCU01- Customer Sales Area

Step 3: Defining Number Ranges for Business Partner

Path : SPRO>> IMG>>Cross Application Components>>SAP Business Partner>>Business Partner>>Basic Settings>> Number Ranges and Groupings - Define Number Ranges.

New Number Range is created.

Note : The External Check box is Unchecked, BP Number will be generated based on the number range interval.

Step 4 : Defining BP Grouping

Path : SPRO>> IMG>>Cross Application Components>>SAP Business Partner>>Business Partner>>Basic Settings>> Number Ranges and Groupings - Define Groupings and Assign Number Ranges.

New BP Grouping is created and Assigned to the number range defined above

Step 5: Define Customer Account Group

Path: SPRO>>IMG>>Financial Accounting>>Accounts Receivable and Accounts Payable>>Customer Accounts>>Master Data>>Preparation for Creating Customer Master Data>> Define Account Groups With screen layout

Customer Account Groups are created for SP, SH, BP, and PY Partner Functions.

Step 6: Define Number Ranges for Customer Account Group

Path: SPRO>>IMG>>Financial Accounting>>Accounts Receivable and Accounts Payable>>Customer Accounts>>Master Data>>Preparation for Creating Customer Master Data>>Create Number Ranges for Customer Accounts

 

Number Ranges for Customer Account Groups are created, and the External Check Box is selected. The Number for the Customer role (ZFCU00 and ZFCU01) will be the same number determined for the BP role ZGG000.

Note: The Number Ranges of Customer Account Group should be the same as the Number Ranges for Business Partner

Step 7: Assign the Number Range to the Customer Account Group

Path: SPRO>>IMG>>Financial Accounting>>Accounts Receivable and Accounts Payable>>Customer Accounts>>Master Data>>Preparation for Creating Customer Master Data>>Create Number Ranges for Customer Accounts

Assigning the number ranges to the Customer Account Groups

Step 8 : Master Data Synchronization

Assign the Customer Account Group to BP Grouping

Path: SPRO>> IMG>>Cross Application Components>>SAP Business Partner>>Master Data Synchronization>>Customer/Vendor Integration>>Business Partner Settings>>Settings for Customer integration>>Define BP role for Direction Customer to BP

Assign the Number Assignment for Direction BP to the Customer.

Path: SPRO>> IMG>>Cross Application Components>>SAP Business Partner>>Master Data Synchronization>>Customer/Vendor Integration>>Business Partner Settings>>Settings for Customer integration>>Field Assignment for Customer Integration>>Assign Keys>>Define Number Assignment for Direction BP to Customer.

Note: The Same Number Check box needs to be checked to have the Same Number in BP and Customer.

Step 9 : Partner Determination

The New Customer Account Groups created should be assigned with the partner Determination procedure.

Path: SPRO>>IMG>>Sales and Distribution>>Basic Functions>>Partner Determination>>Setup Partner Determination- Setup Partner Determination for Customer and Supplier Master

9.1 Define New Partner Determination Procedure

Path: SPRO>>IMG>>Sales and Distribution>>Basic Functions>>Partner Determination>>Setup Partner Determination- Setup Partner Determination for Customer and Supplier Master - Partner Determination Procedure

9.2 Assign Partner Functions to the Partner Determination Procedure

Path: SPRO>>IMG>>Sales and Distribution>>Basic Functions>>Partner Determination>>Setup Partner Determination- Setup Partner Determination for Customer and Supplier Master - Partner Functions in Procedure

9.3 Assign the Partner Determination procedure to customer Account Group

Path: SPRO>>IMG>>Sales and Distribution>>Basic Functions>>Partner Determination>>Setup Partner Determination- Setup Partner Determination for Customer and Supplier Master - Partner Determination Procedure Assignment.

Configuration of the Business Partner With the Same number for BP and Customer is completed. Now we will create a new BP and check the configurations.

Creation of a Business Partner

Step 1: Select the Transaction BP and click on create Organization. Select the BP Grouping "ZBPG". Provide

The General role for the BP is created, a new entry in the Table BUT000 is created upon successfully saving the BP

Step 2 : Click on the Change Mode in BP Screen and enter the BP Number "2213" and select the role "ZFCU00" to create the customer.

Customer Master is created with the same Number as BP, and the Customer Master table is updated with the company code data and General Data.

KNA1 Table - General Data

KNB1 Table - Company Code Data

CVI_CUST_LINK table is also updated.

Step 3 : Click on the Change Mode in BP Screen and enter the BP Number "2213" and select the role "ZFCU01" to create the customer sales Area Data.

Sales Area Table for the Customer is updated.

entry.title: Want to Process the Sales orders against the particular MRP only
entry.content:
Currently, during Sales Order (SO) creation in SAP, the system automatically selects the first available MRP (based on date/month) and deducts quantity from it. This leads to incorrect stock consumption and affects Raw Material (RM) planning.

We require the ability to manually select the MRP record (product-wise, month-wise, or project-wise) during SO creation. The system should then consume quantity only from the selected MRP and not affect any other MRP record. This is necessary to ensure accurate RM requirement planning and avoid consumption mismatches.
